title: Tiering Your Accounts - The 'Sniper vs. Shotgun' Method

questions.question: What is the 'Peanut Butter Mistake' in sales prospecting?

options.text: Spreading your sales effort evenly across all prospects, regardless of their size or potential.
Sending gifts that people are allergic to.

questions.explanation: Treating a 500-person company the same as a 5-person company is inefficient. You must allocate resources based on potential value.

questions.question: Which strategy describes a 'Tier 1' Account approach?

options.text: The Sniper: 100% Manual, Deep Research, Hyper-personalized.
The Hybrid: Automated template with a custom first line.

questions.explanation: Tier 1 accounts are your 'Must Win' high-value targets. They require deep research (podcasts, 10-Ks) and zero automation.

questions.question: What is the appropriate volume for 'Tier 3' accounts?

options.text: The bottom 60% of your list (High volume).

questions.explanation: Tier 3 accounts are 'Good Enough' fits but low strategic value. Use automation (The Shotgun) to handle them efficiently at volume.

questions.question: Why should you listen to a podcast a CEO was on before emailing them (Tier 1)?

options.text: To find a specific quote to prove you did your homework and aren't a bot.
To see if they have a nice voice.

questions.explanation: Quoting a specific insight from a podcast (e.g. at minute 20) is the ultimate proof of work. It builds instant trust.

questions.question: What happens if you spend 'Tier 1' effort on a 'Tier 3' account?

options.text: You waste expensive time on a low-value opportunity, destroying your unit economics.

questions.explanation: Resource allocation is key. Spending 30 minutes researching a tiny account is bad business, even if you win the deal.

questions.question: Create a simple 3-tier account classification for your target market. What criteria determine whether a prospect is Tier 1 (high-touch), Tier 2 (medium), or Tier 3 (low-touch)?
questions.promptForAI: Evaluate the tiering criteria. Check for: (1) clear differentiation between tiers, (2) practical criteria, (3) resource allocation logic. Suggest improvements to tiering strategy.
